title: Fix Admin URL Access — Allow Shared Admin Links to Open Admin Panel

requirements.text: When a user navigates to the admin page URL directly (e.g., via a shared link), they must be shown the admin panel (PIN auth guard) instead of being redirected to the main app or login page. The router must not intercept or redirect away from the /admin route for users who are not yet authenticated via Internet Identity — instead, the admin route should render the PINAuthGuard component which handles its own authentication flow independently of the main app auth.

quotes: If am forwarding my admin url link to second person but they are unable to open admin panel there showing direct main app

acceptanceCriteria: Navigating directly to the /admin URL (e.g., via a shared link) renders the admin panel PIN guard screen, not the main app feed or login page.
A user who has not logged in via Internet Identity can still reach the admin PIN prompt when accessing the admin URL directly.
The Layout component's authentication redirect logic does not redirect /admin route visitors away from the admin page.
After entering the correct PIN on the admin panel, the full admin interface is shown regardless of Internet Identity login state.
All other protected routes (Mix, Poetry, Dua, Songs) continue to redirect unauthenticated users to the login page as before.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
The existing PINAuthGuard component must remain the access control mechanism for the admin panel.
Do not remove or weaken existing authentication/ban checks for non-admin routes.
nonGoals: Adding Internet Identity login requirement to access the admin panel.
Changing the PIN authentication logic inside PINAuthGuard.
Modifying backend access control or admin-related backend functions.
Adding role-based access control or new user permission models.
